differential_diagnoses: Chronic extrahepatic portal vein thrombosis secondary to abdominal trauma
Mesenteric angina (chronic mesenteric ischemia) due to vascular flow redistribution
Portomesenteric venous thrombosis with intestinal congestion
Budd-Chiari syndrome (ruled out by Doppler, but consideration for rare anatomical variants)
Occult intra-abdominal malignancy contributing to portal venous occlusion
next_steps: CT angiography of the mesenteric arteries and veins to assess for chronic mesenteric ischemia and detailed vascular anatomy
Serologic testing for Schistosoma mansoni and review of peripheral blood eosinophil count
Measurement of hepatic venous pressure gradient (HVPG) to confirm pre-sinusoidal portal hypertension
Consultation with vascular surgery and interventional radiology for potential revascularization or decompression options
Continued medical management of varices with beta-blockers and proton pump inhibitors
